business_idea: BookSlot — a dead-simple $19/month booking page for local service businesses (salons, clinics, tutors, mechanics, coaches) who currently manage appointments via WhatsApp chaos. The owner gets a shareable link (bookslot.app/their-name), customers book themselves, and the owner gets a WhatsApp/email notification with the booking details. No accounts needed for customers. Bilingual (English + Arabic/French) from day one to target the underserved MENA and North Africa market. MVP has: (1) owner signup + service setup page, (2) public booking page, (3) time slot availability, (4) email confirmation to both parties, (5) a simple dashboard showing upcoming bookings. Monetization: free for up to 20 bookings/month, $19/month for unlimited. Target: solo service providers who have 10-50 clients/week and are drowning in WhatsApp messages.
next_task: Create the full project scaffold: a FastAPI backend (main.py) with SQLite database (via SQLAlchemy) and 3 models: Owner (id, name, email, business_name, slug, services_json, availability_json, created_at), Booking (id, owner_id, customer_name, customer_email, customer_phone, service, datetime, status, created_at), and Settings (id, owner_id, timezone, language). Add Alembic for migrations. Create a requirements.txt with: fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy, alembic, pydantic, python-jose, passlib, python-multipart, jinja2, aiosmtplib. Add a basic src/main.py that starts the app with CORS enabled and a GET /health endpoint that returns {status: ok, version: 0.1.0}.
notes: Product: BookSlot. Target market: MENA/North Africa local service businesses. Key differentiators: (1) bilingual Arabic+French+English, (2) WhatsApp-native notification output, (3) $19/mo — undercuts all competitors, (4) zero friction for customers (no signup). Stack decision: FastAPI + SQLite (simple, no external DB needed for MVP) + Jinja2 templates for frontend (no build step, simpler for agent to manage). Auth: simple JWT for owner login. Do NOT use React or Node — keep it pure Python + HTML/CSS for simplicity. Each owner gets a unique slug like /book/sara-salon. The booking page must be beautiful and mobile-first because customers will open it from WhatsApp on their phones.
target_market: MENA + North Africa local service businesses
roadmap: iteration_01_05: Project scaffold, DB models, health endpoint
iteration_06_10: Owner signup/login, service + availability setup
iteration_31_35: Polish, error handling, README, deploy config
